title: [SV] Spilling Out of Procedural Region can produce Xs with disallowLocalVariables
body:
If `-lowering-options=disallowLocalVariables` is used to generate Verilog, you can create situations where registers are not randomized in the evaluation of the initial block.

Consider the following source FIRRTL:

```scala
circuit Foo:
  module Foo:
    input clock: Clock
    input d: UInt<1>
    output q: UInt<1>

    reg r: UInt<1>, clock

    r <= d
    q <= r
```

This has a register smaller than 32-bit and will result in a bit select off of a `RANDOM` verbatim expression.  This will eventually produce the following Verilog using `firtool -verilog -lowering-options=disallowLocalVariables`:

```verilog
module Foo(	// Foo.fir:2:10
  input  clock, d,
  output q);

  reg r;	// Foo.fir:7:5

  `ifndef SYNTHESIS	// Foo.fir:7:5
    reg [31:0] _T;	// Foo.fir:7:5

  wire _T_0 = _T[0];	// Foo.fir:7:5
    initial begin	// Foo.fir:7:5
      `INIT_RANDOM_PROLOG_	// Foo.fir:7:5
      `ifdef RANDOMIZE_REG_INIT	// Foo.fir:7:5
        _T = `RANDOM;	// Foo.fir:7:5
        r = _T_0;	// Foo.fir:7:5
      `endif
    end // initial
  `endif
  always @(posedge clock)	// Foo.fir:9:7
    r <= d;	// Foo.fir:9:7
  assign q = r;	// Foo.fir:2:10
endmodule
```

The spilling of the wire out of the procedural initial region is wrong as far as I can tell.  What happens is this:

1. `_T` gets a random value
2. `r` gets the value of `_T_0` (which is currently `x`) and has not been updated to `_T[0]`
3. `_T_0` gets updated

This is tedious because we really can't spill outside of this procedural region without changing semantics.

For comparison, the SFC produces:
```verilog
module Foo(
  input   clock,
  input   d,
  output  q
);
`ifdef RANDOMIZE_REG_INIT
  reg [31:0] _RAND_0;
`endif // RANDOMIZE_REG_INIT
  reg  r;
  assign q = r;
  always @(posedge clock) begin
    r <= d;
  end
// Register and memory initialization
`ifdef RANDOMIZE_GARBAGE_ASSIGN
`define RANDOMIZE
`endif
`ifdef RANDOMIZE_INVALID_ASSIGN
`define RANDOMIZE
`endif
`ifdef RANDOMIZE_REG_INIT
`define RANDOMIZE
`endif
`ifdef RANDOMIZE_MEM_INIT
`define RANDOMIZE
`endif
`ifndef RANDOM
`define RANDOM $random
`endif
`ifdef RANDOMIZE_MEM_INIT
  integer initvar;
`endif
`ifndef SYNTHESIS
`ifdef FIRRTL_BEFORE_INITIAL
`FIRRTL_BEFORE_INITIAL
`endif
initial begin
  `ifdef RANDOMIZE
    `ifdef INIT_RANDOM
      `INIT_RANDOM
    `endif
    `ifndef VERILATOR
      `ifdef RANDOMIZE_DELAY
        #`RANDOMIZE_DELAY begin end
      `else
        #0.002 begin end
      `endif
    `endif
`ifdef RANDOMIZE_REG_INIT
  _RAND_0 = {1{`RANDOM}};
  r = _RAND_0[0:0];
`endif // RANDOMIZE_REG_INIT
  `endif // RANDOMIZE
end // initial
`ifdef FIRRTL_AFTER_INITIAL
`FIRRTL_AFTER_INITIAL
`endif
`endif // SYNTHESIS
endmodule
```
